findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "take_energy_leak_quiz" is 2307 chars, far longer than this server's median (150). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Score the user's Energy Leak quiz answers and return the winning leak plus the full 5-1-1 protocol. Answer all seven questions. q1: It's 3pm. What's your energy…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "subscribe_to_outliyr" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

confidence.title: Description uses pressure/override language
confidence.detail: Description of "take_energy_leak_quiz" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Score the user's Energy Leak quiz answers and return the winning leak plus the full 5-1-1 protocol. Answer all seven questions. q1: It's 3pm. What's your energy…

confidence.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
confidence.detail: Tool "subscribe_to_outliyr" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

confidence.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
confidence.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: subscribe_to_outliyr · Public sink: subscribe_to_outliyr.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: subscribe_to_outliyr · Public sink: subscribe_to_outliyr

confidence.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
